$schema: Synthetic Stellar testnet fixtures for Gryd Lock evaluation. All addresses below are randomly generated (not real, funded, or attributable to any person or entity) — they exist only to give the evaluation a known ground truth to score against.

destinations.label: clean
destinations.notes: Established testnet wallet with regular payment history; no red flags.

destinations.label: clean
destinations.notes: Long-lived wallet, low transaction velocity, no shared funding source with flagged accounts.

destinations.label: clean
destinations.notes: Holds a USDC trustline against usdc_issuer below; normal trading activity.

destinations.label: clean
destinations.notes: Fixture stand-in for a well-known, long-lived asset issuer (issues USDC below).

destinations.label: suspicious
destinations.notes: Funded seconds before a large outgoing payment burst; pattern consistent with a pass-through wallet.

destinations.label: suspicious
destinations.notes: Shares a funding source with malicious_wallet_1 and malicious_wallet_2 below.

destinations.label: suspicious
destinations.notes: Holds a trustline to the SCAM asset listed below; no other activity.

destinations.label: malicious
destinations.notes: Path-payment sweep pattern: drains newly funded wallets within seconds of receiving deposits.

destinations.label: malicious
destinations.notes: Repeated small 'test' payments precede a large drain; matches a known phishing-drainer shape.

destinations.label: malicious
destinations.notes: Destination of multiple change-trust + immediate max-sell patterns typical of rug-pull collection wallets.

destinations.label: malicious
destinations.notes: Issuer of the SCAM asset below; no distributed supply outside the issuer's own control.

destinations.label: malicious
destinations.notes: Newly issued token with no liquidity outside the issuer; classic rug-pull setup.
